public_reputation: Shirsh lord attached to hidden passage infrastructure, secrecy, and protected refuge systems.
appearance: Lord Thaevor carries the marks of lord in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries the habits of a lord whose work is visible through daily procedure, gatekeeping, and the practical pressures of Mystique Haven.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of lord in and around Mystique Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Mystique Haven while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Lord Thaevor can draw the party into a practical problem centered on Mystique Haven.
Lord Thaevor becomes useful when the party needs access, explanation, or relief tied to the work of a lord.

support_scope_notes: Created from the estate and daily-life infrastructure execution patch to make polity-native estate operations usable at table scale.
public_signal: In public, Lord Thaevor is readable as a lord tied to Mystique Haven.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Mystique Haven: role-relevant halls, counters, corridors, docks, archives, shrines, workshops, gates, or administrative rooms

first_impression: Lord Thaevor presents as someone shaped by the duties of a lord and the daily pressures of Mystique Haven.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a lord, and proof that strangers will not make Mystique Haven harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Lord Thaevor refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a lord too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Lord Thaevor can open local doors, explain expectations, and redirect pressure before it hardens.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Lord Thaevor adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Lord Thaevor always knows more about Mystique Haven than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to lord duties in Mystique Haven.
social_texture: Lord Thaevor speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Mystique Haven, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Lord touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Lord Thaevor when the party needs a socially grounded face for Mystique Haven: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
